title: Performance, Load, Supply-Chain Gates, and Production Client Defense · Go Client Systems · Go Semantics Case: Latency Throughput Budget
summary: A production Go integration must defend latency, memory, concurrency, dependency provenance, rollback, and behavior under doubled load. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http go latency throughput budget increment in a Go 1.26 net/http production HTTP client release dossier. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Measure latency distributions, queue time, throughput, error rate, and saturation against an end-to-end service objective. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Record stable client duration, method, route or safe target, status, error type, attempts, bytes, and trace context without high-cardinality secrets. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go Predict Latency Throughput Budget · Theory Latency Throughput Budget
steps.instruction:
theory case — Go fleet evidence model release G-4 doubles traffic and body size while p95 latency, memory, retries, and queue depth rise. Measure the bottleneck, preserve behavior, verify supply-chain gates and rollback, then defend production readiness. Competency probe: Measure latency distributions, queue time, throughput, error rate, and saturation against an end-to-end service objective. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
